dialogues.value: Welcome little explorers! Today, we're going on an exciting journey through the jungle. Get ready to discover new things and learn about needs, wants, bartering,  goods, and services!

sections.value:
Are you ready to help Ray, Max, and Ms.
Greenline get ready for their safari?

Pack their bags with essential items and
make sure they have everything they
would require for a fun and safe journey!

contents.value:
Select 5 things that you think
are essential for the safari.

dialogues.value: But, I want to bring all my favourite things to this adventure.
Ray, when we're on a jungle safari, there are some things that are super-duper important and we call them needs.

sections.value:
A need is something that is
necessary for your survival.

question.value:
Pick the essentials for your
jungle safari.

sections.value: When you have a desire to possess or do something that you wish for, it is referred as a “WANT”.
Simply put, Wants are the things that you would like to have but don’t need.

data.text: Hello Ms. Greenline. Can you give me one-word answer on which is a need out of video game, bicycle, stationery, and clay for a student.
data.context: If you need help Champ, let's ask Ms. Greenline.

dialogues.text: Just as the safari, in life, we need to prioritize our needs and then work towards our wants.

sections.value:
As Ray, Max, and Ms. Greenline explore
the jungle, they ate up all their food and
find themselves hungry.

Soon after, they stumble upon, Mr. Smith
(a villager) with a bundle of wooden
planks.

dialogues.value: Max, my stomach is growling louder than the jungle sounds. Let's ask Mr. Smith for some wooden planks so that we can cook food.
Agreed, we can definitely propose a trade.
Always good to ask, champs. But in, these parts, people often trade for what they give – it's called bartering.

sections.value: Trading/Exchanging services or goods with another person.
Back in the olden days, people used to exchange goods and services directly for other goods and services without the use of money.
Trading/Exchanging services or goods with another person.
Back in the olden days, people used to exchange goods and services directly for other goods and services without the use of money.

dialogues.text: Good going, Champ! Now, it's time to watch a fun video and understand “Bartering”

contents.value:
Select the option that describes
barter system!

It is something that we use nowadays to buy
things, like toys or candy.

It is when people exchange things they have for
things they want without using money.

dialogues.value: Okay. So, what do we give in return for the wooden planks?
It depends on what Mr. Smith needs. You can ask him what he would like to take from your backpacks.

contents.value:
Click on all the items that
can be traded.

incorrect.value: I don't need a bug spray, I have one.
What would I do with a tent? It is of no use to me.
I have already applied sunscreen, I don't need this.

sections.value:
Seems like Mr. Smith does not want
any of these items.

dialogues.value: I couldn't find anything I need, but you know what kids? You can have the wooden planks for free. Consider it as a gift.
Max, I feel kind of bad for taking the wooden planks for free. We should ask him if he needs something.
Hello, Mr. Smith. We'd like to do something for you in return for the wooden planks. Is there anything you need help with?
Well, if you're offering, I could use some help with crossing the river bridge with these wooden planks.
Great! So you kids finally found a way to complete the barter.
Huh? How’s that possible? We did not give him anything in return.

dialogues.text: Well! You performed an action which is called as a “service”, and in return, you got the log. That's a form of bartering. You don't always need to exchange goods; sometimes a helpful service is just as valuable.

sections.value: A good is a physical item that can be bought, touched, and used.
A service is the action done for people who pay for the service.

dialogues.text: Good going, Champ! Now, it's time to watch another fun video and understand “goods and services”.

sections.value:
Imagine that you have ordered a pizza
and soon after, the delivery person
comes to deliver it to you.

dialogues.value: Ms. Greenline, thank you for guiding us on this amazing jungle safari. It was incredible!
Yeah, Ms. Greenline, you made it so much fun! What can we give you in return for this awesome service?

contents.value:
I'm not a necessity,
just something fun,
Like a toy or a game
when the day is done.

In the past, before money,
we used to trade,
No coins, no bills,
just goods swapped, not paid.

I come in many forms, big and small,
From a toy car to a basketball.
You can hold me, use me, and sometimes share, Without me, life's dull, I add joy everywhere!

Select the service from the
options below

title.value: Did you have fun in this learning module? *

content.value:
A bit confusing, 
but I figured it out.

title.value: What made the module feel this way? *

titleSummary.value: Choose as many as you like

content.value: I needed more help to understand.

content.value: Kind of boring, needed more action.
A bit, but some parts were puzzling.

content.value: A bit tricky, but I got it!
Super easy, I got it all!

content.value: It went too fast for me.
